# Locations — Index

One file per location in this folder (copy `00_location_template.md`). Add a new file as new locations appear in stories. Per-location operating rules live in each file; cross-cutting rules and geography live below.

This index carries the **hard rules** — the facts that make a scene checkable. A location file without operating constraints is just set dressing; the constraints are what catch continuity errors.

---

## All Locations

**[Category — e.g., Homes / gathering spaces]:** [Location A](location-a.md) · [Location B](location-b.md)
**[Category — e.g., Recurring public places]:** …
**[Category — e.g., Story-specific]:** …

## Canon Facts to Maintain (geography)

Facts about where things are relative to each other. Every stated distance or adjacency becomes a constraint on future scenes.

- [e.g., Character X lives walking distance from Location Y]
- [e.g., The two districts are on opposite sides of the river]

## Canon Rules: Schedules & Operating Constraints

Hard rules. If a scene violates one of these, the scene is wrong — not the rule.

- **[Recurring event]:** [e.g., The market runs Sunday only, 9am–2pm. A character cannot be there on a Saturday; on any other day the stalls are empty.]
- **[A character's availability]:** [e.g., X works variable shifts — keep their availability flexible rather than pinning a fixed schedule you'll have to honor forever.]
- **Travel times (approx):** [List the canonical durations between recurring locations. These are what make "no two places at once" checkable.]
- **No two places at once:** a character cannot be at two locations in less time than travel allows.
